description.value:
Asmodeus is said to have been among the earliest divine intelligences to shape matter into ordered form. Where other gods explored creation through curiosity, variation, and impermanence, Asmodeus pursued perfection as a fixed state.

He believed creation was not meant to be diverse, but correct. In his doctrine, there was an ideal form of life—an apex design that all things should aspire to become or be replaced by.

His greatest work was the attempt to forge a race in his own image: not merely powerful, but refined, structured, and absolute in purpose. However, as his vision hardened, so too did his judgment. All other divine creations became, in his eyes, flawed drafts of reality—unfinished and therefore disposable.

This belief seeped into his devils, who came to interpret “perfection” as entitlement. If they were the most refined creation, then dominion over lesser forms was not conquest, but correction.
